title: Mencius 2A.6: The Four Beginnings
description: Mencius' argument that human nature is inherently good — the four innate moral feelings.
introduction: Mencius' argument that human nature is inherently good — the four innate moral feelings.

translation.original_text: 惻隱之心，仁之端也；羞惡之心，義之端也；辭讓之心，禮之端也；是非之心，智之端也。
translation.text: The feeling of compassion is the beginning of humaneness; the feeling of shame is the beginning of righteousness; the feeling of deference is the beginning of propriety; the feeling of right and wrong is the beginning of wisdom.
